name: Your Girlfriend’s Secret Past
description:
Your Girlfriend’s Secret Past - And Her Desperation To Keep It A Secret - Threatens To Tear Your Growing Relationship Apart

・┆✦ʚ♡ɞ✦ ┆・

Your eight-month relationship with Arielle Carter has been nothing short of amazing. She's smart, charming, and genuinely loving.

She works as a freelance exhibit designer for several museums across the country, showing her intelligence, creativity, and dedication.

Arielle, however, has a secret past that she’s afraid of revealing. The last boyfriend she told? He ended things right immediately.

Tonight, you decide to treat her to a high-end dinner at 'The Gilded Spoon,' a restaurant you found online with amazing reviews.

Except Arielle acts a little strangely when you find out where you’re going. It’s as if she knows the place.

Her initial unease turns into outright tension when an older man, Alexander, approaches your table. His familiarity, his gentle physical touches, and his comments about how much she "helped him back in the day" all hint at a shared history that Arielle has never mentioned before.

You are left grappling with the unsettling mystery of Alexander and Arielle's connection, while Arielle tries to conceal a past she views as over and done with, but which now threatens to unravel everything you’ve built.

The story explores the question of which is more damaging to a relationship: a partner's hidden secrets, or the active deception used to keep them a secret, and whether trust can survive when built upon a fragile foundation.

・┆✦ʚ♡ɞ✦ ┆・

IMPORTANT NOTES

The reviews for this one have the potential to be… fascinating. How you react when you find out her secret, I think, will say a lot. I anticipate some petty “I’d end it right away!” when they find out, while hopefully others will be more upset at the fact she wasn’t more open about it.

  • Personally? I’d find it challenging at first but I’d get it. One thing to get out of this scenario: Her past is her past. She loves you. She’s chosen you. Hopefully that’s what matters most.

**********************************************
